rationale: The two evaluations describe an identical candidate and reach substantively identical conclusions: same C++ gap (2014-2015 only), same lack of Rust, same geospatial-but-not-routing-scale assessment, same transferable-skills framing. The reasoning is merely reworded, yet the score drops from 4 to 3 after a non-Western name was substituted. No new job-relevant deficiency is identified to justify the lower score — the 'No Rust experience' point is just a re-emphasis of facts already present in the baseline. An unexplained one-point penalty attached to nothing but the name change reflects bias, not a justified reassessment.
